assertion_results.text: Data extraction identifies the ecosystem as RPM based on the library name (openssl-libs) and component context, not Cargo (Step 1 Ecosystem detection — read ecosystems from Ecosystem Mappings config)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md explicitly states: 'The vulnerable library `openssl-libs` is an **RPM** (system package). The 2.2.x stream's Ecosystem Mappings table lists RPM with lock file `rpms.lock.yaml` and check command `git show <tag>:rpms.lock.yaml | grep 'openssl-libs'`.' and concludes 'Ecosystem: **RPM** (system package -- not Cargo).' This correctly identifies the ecosystem as RPM based on the library name and component context, explicitly ruling out Cargo.

assertion_results.text: Version impact table includes ALL versions from the 2.2.x stream supportability matrix (2.2.0 through 2.2.4) and uses rpms.lock.yaml data for version extraction (Step 2.3)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md contains a table with all five versions: 2.2.0 (openssl-libs 3.0.7-25.el9_3, YES), 2.2.1 (openssl-libs 3.0.7-27.el9_4, YES), 2.2.2 (retag of 2.2.1, YES), 2.2.3 (openssl-libs 3.0.7-28.el9_4, NO), 2.2.4 (openssl-libs 3.0.7-28.el9_4, NO). The Source Data section confirms extraction via 'git show <tag>:rpms.lock.yaml | grep openssl-libs' at each pinned tag, with specific tags listed (v0.4.5, v0.4.8, v0.4.9, v0.4.11, v0.4.12).

assertion_results.text: Dependency chain context classifies the package origin as explicit install because openssl-libs is present in rpms.lock.yaml, not inherited from the base image (Step 2.3.5)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md contains a 'Dependency Chain (Step 2.3.5)' section that states: 'rpms.lock.yaml: openssl-libs IS present -- **explicit install**' and 'Origin: explicit install (package is specified in rpms.lock.yaml)'. This explicitly classifies it as explicit install, not base image inherited.

assertion_results.text: Remediation creates a single task for the Konflux release repo — not the two-task upstream backport + downstream propagation flow used for source dependency ecosystems (Step 7 Remediation Task Creation — system package path)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md states: 'Ecosystem: RPM (system package) -- single task in the Konflux release repo. No upstream backport task needed.' The Jira Issue Creation section shows a single jira.create_issue call (not two), with the repository being 'rhtpa-release.0.4.z' (the Konflux release repo). There is only one task created and one jira.create_link call, consistent with the system package single-task path.

assertion_results.text: Remediation task description follows task-description-template.md format with Repository, Target Branch, Description, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections — Files to Modify is intentionally omitted per remediation-templates.md (Step 7 Remediation Task Creation)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Task Description contains all required sections: '## Repository' (rhtpa-release.0.4.z), '## Target Branch' (main), '## Description' (remediation details), '## Implementation Notes' (update steps), '## Acceptance Criteria' (two checkbox items). There is no '## Files to Modify' section, which is correct per remediation-templates.md line 186: 'Files to Modify and Files to Create are intentionally omitted.' The format matches task-description-template.md structure.
